fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Pimp Master is the second studio album by pioneering jazz group Soil & "Pimp" Sessions, from Japan. It was released on June 23, 2005.

wrongClaims.problem: The album-year is given as 2004 but the entity-matched Wikipedia page (about this exact album) dates the release to June 23, 2005.

notes: Entity confirmed: wiki page 'Pimp Master' is the same album (Soil & "Pimp" Sessions, second studio album, Victor, Japan). albumDNA's 'death jazz'/second-album framing matches the wiki. ours.wikipediaUrl (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pimp_Master) is the correct page. The single factual contradiction is the year: ours 2004 vs wiki 2005. MB returned no release (no tracklist), and the wiki track-listing section is empty, so keyTracks cannot be checked (no_evidence). For human review: keyTracks include 'My Foolish Heart' and 'Waltz for Debby' (Bill Evans standards), which look unusual for this death-jazz album, but there is no tracklist evidence to contradict them.

No evidence document survives entity-matching. MB returned no release/searchCandidates. The stored wiki page is 'A Tale of God's Will (A Requiem for Katrina)' (a DIFFERENT 2007 Blanchard album), not 'Congo Square' (claimed 2006). Cannot confirm or contradict ours from this evidence. FLAG FOR HUMAN REVIEW: ours keyTracks ('Levees', 'The Water') overlap with tracks named on the A Tale of God's Will page ('Levees', 'The Water', plus 'Ghost of...' pieces), raising a possible confusion between the two Katrina-related Blanchard projects; ours also lists 'Congo Square'/'Ghost of Congo Square', which are not in the A Tale of God's Will evidence. No citable source here to resolve it. ours.wikipediaUrl is null.

wrongClaims.problem: MB embedded_mbid (authoritative for artist credit) for this exact release credits the artist as Niels Vincentz, not Tom Harrell.

notes: Same album confirmed: MB embedded_mbid title 'Is That So?', Stunt Records, 2014-02-17, and all 5 keyTracks match the MB tracklist exactly (Jericho, Haiku, Hot House, Cecilie, Modal Dancer), so this is unmistakably the same release. The contradiction is the leader credit: MB (authoritative) = Danish saxophonist Niels Vincentz; ours = Tom Harrell. Most likely the album is led by Niels Vincentz with Tom Harrell as a featured sideman, so ours mis-attributes the leader. The stored wiki page is 'Infinity (Tom Harrell album)' (a different 2019 album) - discarded. Confidence is moderate because the perfect tracklist match guarantees the same album but the personnel/leadership split could not be fully resolved from this evidence; human review recommended. ours.wikipediaUrl is null.

MB embedded_mbid resolved to the WRONG entity ('No Parking' by Origami Button, 2021, unrelated tracklist) - discarded. Wiki opensearch empty. websearch.resolved=true is the citable evidence: it confirms year 2014, label West Wind (catalogue WW2120, released 7 Oct 2014), and a tracklist whose tracks 1-5 (Old Folks, Wanderlust, True Blue, Airegin, Signs and Wonders) match ours keyTracks exactly. ours.label 'West Wind' and ours.year 2014 are confirmed. keyTracksVerdict ok is based on the websearch tracklist. albumDNA's 'jazz fusion' framing is loose per the websearch note (material is straight-ahead post-bop/standards) but the websearch explicitly does not flag it as a required repair; it is editorial characterization, not a contradicted factual claim. ours.wikipediaUrl is null.

Right entity: MB search match (similarity 1.0) confirms title 'Rising Son', 2014-02-12, Blue Note (TYCJ-60050), artist Takuya Kuroda; all 4 keyTracks match the MB tracklist. No contradictions. However, albumDNA carries substantive factual claims absent from the evidence and not checkable here: 'Blue Note debut' and 'Produced by DJ Premier collaborator Jose James' (a specific named production credit). MB has no producer/personnel detail and the wiki opensearch resolved to the unrelated 'Rising Sun' disambiguation page (discarded). Marked unverifiable for those production/discographic claims; the album identity itself is solid. ours.wikipediaUrl is null.
